zh.County Meath, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯
zh.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite於大約1250年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.County Meath, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 米亞斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite于大约1250年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 米亞斯。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Tuite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Conan.
Irish (Leinster; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places called Le Thuit in Eure, France, named with Old Norse thveit ‘meadow’. Compare Thwaites .

variants.name: Thwaites
meanings: English: from the northern English placename Thwaite (Old Norse thveit ‘meadow, paddock’) + plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. The plural form is found in several placenames, including Thwaites in Keighley (Yorkshire), which is a principal source of the surname.

variants.name: Tweed
meanings: Irish (Antrim; of Norman origin): variant of   Tuite  .
English (mainly East Anglia): sometimes a variant of   Thwaites  , or   Twite  .
Scottish: probably a shortened form of   Tweddle  .

variants.name: Tuitt
meanings: Irish (of Norman origin): variant of   Tuite  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite was born about 1250, in County Meath, Ireland. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite est né environ 1250, à County Meath, Irlande. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite wurde um 1250 in County Meath, Irland geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite è nato il circa 1250 nel County Meath, Irlanda. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.County Meath, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ メアス
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Tuiteは約1250年にアイルランドコウントイ メアスで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite(은)는 대략 1250년 아일랜드 카운티 미스에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite nasceu aproximadamente 1250, em County Meath, Irlanda. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite родился около 1250 г. в местности коунти мит, Ирландия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Tuite nació aproximadamente 1250, en County Meath, Irlanda. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
